test(game): add fuzz tests with randomized configs

50 random full-game simulations with varying player
count (2-6), map shape, grid type, fog of war,
power-ups, alliances, and personality mixes including
custom configs. Plus 7 edge case tests. Seeds printed
on failure for reproducibility.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(ai): add custom AI personality builder

Add ability to create custom AI personalities with adjustable parameters:
- Min Advantage slider (-2 to +4)
- Max Attacks slider (1 to 10, or unlimited)
- Connectivity Bonus slider (0 to 5)

Includes named preset save/load/delete via localStorage, popup editor
in SetupScene, and full integration with GameScene player creation.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(game): change Charge power-up from flat +3 bonus to +2 extra dice

Charge now adds 2 extra dice to the attacker's roll instead of a flat +3
to the total. This lets territories with 8 dice roll 10 dice on attack,
making Charge meaningfully powerful at high dice counts.

- Rename POWER_UP_BONUS to CHARGE_EXTRA_DICE (2) and SHIELD_DEFENSE_BONUS (3)
- Roll extra dice before summing instead of adding to total after
- Update AI advantage calculation to use +2 for charge
- Shield remains as flat +3 defender bonus (passive defense)
- Add new tests for extra dice rolling and power-up consumption
- Update GAME_LOGIC.md documentation
- Replace simulation seed 123 (stalemates under new balance)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: mark phase-11 defect ticket as done

All 8 bugs fixed: seed degeneration, self-alliance,
duplicate elimination, dead alliance cleanup,
graph perf, surrender awareness, underdog logic,
undo alliance state. Plus undo config feature.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(game): make undo configurable in game setup

Gate undo functionality on undoEnabled config:
- Skip snapshot creation when undo is disabled
- Hide undo button and ignore Z key when disabled
- Remove undo shortcut from help overlay when disabled
- Add tests for undoEnabled persistence and validation

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(game): AI surrender considers power-ups and alliances

hasPositiveAdvantageAttack() now accounts for charge power-up (+2
effective attack dice) and filters out allied targets that the AI
personality would not attack. This prevents premature surrender when
the AI has meaningful tactical options via power-ups or when alliance
restrictions falsely suggest no valid targets exist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(game): prevent duplicate elimination events on human attacks

Capture alive players before executeAttack() and only fire elimination
events for players who were alive before but are now dead. This matches
the pattern already used in AI turn processing and prevents corrupted
achievement tracking (triple_kill, first_blood).

commit.message:
Fix connection leak: use finally blocks to always close DB connections

Every database function now follows the pattern:
- conn = None before try:
- conn = get_connection() as first line inside try:
- all conn.close() calls inside try: removed
- finally: block added to ensure conn.close() is always called

This prevents connection leaks when exceptions occur between
acquiring and closing the connection.

commit.message:
Fix newsstand listing all 30040 indices with broken links (#2)

* Initial plan

* Fix newsstand: add slug key to graph results and filter only type=magazine

Two bugs in GraphMagazineListService:

1. Missing 'slug' key in query results: The template accesses item.slug
   but the graph results only had 'd_tag', causing all links to be /mag/
   without the slug.

2. isTopLevelMagazine() was too permissive: Used OR logic (type=magazine
   OR has 30040 a-tags) instead of requiring the type=magazine tag. This
   caused non-magazine 30040 indices (categories, etc.) to appear in the
   newsstand listing.

commit.message:
Add support for workspace compilation (#2161)

### ❔What, Why & How

<!-- Include description of the changes that will help reviewers in
their task -->

With v28 of BC the ALTool now supports workspace compilation. The key
advantage of workspace compilation is that it supports parallel
compilation which can significantly improve the overall compilation time
for large AL-Go projects.

This PR adds AL-Go support for workspace compilation by adding a new
"Compile" action. Thereby also moving the compilation out of
Run-ALPipeline.

**Limitations**
Limitations that aren't addressed in this PR but are on our radar to fix
in follow-up PRs:
* Support for Directory.packages.props 
* Support for compilation on linux (Status: Bug on Developer
Experiences)
* Support for incremental builds
* Support for compiling BCPT apps
* Support for "Previous Apps" app source validation (i.e. apps from
releases used as baseline)

Related to:
[AB#618073](https://dynamicssmb2.visualstudio.com/1fcb79e7-ab07-432a-a3c6-6cf5a88ba4a5/_workitems/edit/618073)

### ✅ Checklist

- [x] Add tests (E2E, unit tests)
- [x] Update RELEASENOTES.md
- [x] Update documentation (e.g. for new settings or scenarios)
- [x] Add telemetry

<!-- Include more checklist entries, if needed -->

Use GIMP 'Color to Alpha' for pixel-perfect icon edges

Previous approaches (flood fill + blur, nearest-color feather) all
produced visible white fringing because they treated alpha and color
independently.

New approach uses the industry-standard Color to Alpha algorithm
(GIMP/Krita technique):
  alpha = max(|R-white|, |G-white|, |B-white|) / 255
  fg_c  = (observed_c - white*(1-alpha)) / alpha

This is the exact mathematical inverse of alpha compositing over
white. Edge pixels get the correct partial alpha AND the correct
recovered foreground color simultaneously. When re-composited over
white, it reproduces the original source pixel-perfectly.

Applied to flood-filled background + 2px fringe zone. Interior
pixels remain fully opaque (alpha=255).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix white halo: aggressive flood fill + color-corrected feather

Previous Gaussian blur (sigma=1.8) spread alpha into white background
pixels, creating a visible white halo. New approach:
1. Aggressive flood fill (threshold 240) removes ALL white fringe
2. Tiny blur (sigma=0.6) for ~2px edge softening only
3. Newly-transparent edge pixels get RGB from nearest opaque icon
   pixel (via distance_transform_edt), not from original white

Edge profile now: dark green (91,112→54,83) with no white bleed.

Smooth icon edges: Gaussian blur + alpha un-premultiply

Previous approach used a 3px linear alpha ramp which still looked
jagged. New approach:

   alpha gradient at the rounded-rect boundary
2. Un-premultiply RGB at edge pixels to recover true foreground
   color (removes white background bleed-through)
